tagline: Thai funk cassettes, Houston highways, and the sonic threads connecting Houston's globe-trotting trio to a half-century of psychedelic soul.

nodes.name: Suthep Daoduanmai
nodes.connection: Guitarist Mark Speer discovered Thai funk through a worn cassette purchased at a Houston flea market in the early 2000s. Suthep's sinuous, reverb-soaked guitar lines — recorded for the Luk Thung pop circuit in Bangkok — became the harmonic blueprint for Khruangbin's debut LP 'The Universe Smiles Upon You.' Speer has cited these tapes in multiple interviews as the moment the band's sound 'clicked into place.'
nodes.videoTitle: Suthep Daoduanmai — Thai Luk Thung guitar instrumental

nodes.name: The Meters
nodes.connection: Bassist Laura Lee McBride grew up in Houston's R&B and gospel scene, where The Meters were foundational listening. Their locked-in, minimal pocket — one riff repeated until it becomes hypnotic — is the structural engine beneath every Khruangbin groove. The Meters' Art Neville famously said 'we played the same groove, just different versions,' a philosophy Khruangbin absorbed wholesale.

nodes.name: Erykah Badu
nodes.connection: Laura Lee has spoken about Erykah Badu's influence on her understanding of space and silence in bass playing — the idea that what you don't play is as important as what you do. Badu's early records, made twenty minutes from Khruangbin's Houston rehearsal barn, modeled how neo-soul could be simultaneously intimate and vast. The trio opened for Badu in 2019 and described the experience as 'coming full circle.'
nodes.videoTitle: Erykah Badu — On & On (Live at NPR)

nodes.name: Lee "Scratch" Perry
nodes.connection: Khruangbin's production sensibility — heavy reverb, buried vocals, instruments panned wide into a near-empty stereo field — mirrors the dub techniques Lee 'Scratch' Perry pioneered at his Black Ark studio in Kingston. Mark Speer applies cavernous spring reverb to guitar lines in a way that owes direct lineage to Perry's studio experiments. The band's long instrumental passages evoke dub's dissolution of song structure into pure atmosphere.
nodes.videoTitle: Lee Scratch Perry — Super Ape (Full Album)

nodes.name: Curtis Mayfield
nodes.connection: The sweetness at the center of Khruangbin's guitar work — lyrical, falsetto-adjacent melodic lines that float above the rhythm section — traces directly to Curtis Mayfield's revolutionary approach to electric guitar. Mayfield tuned to an open F# chord and played melody as if singing, treating the guitar as a voice rather than a rhythm tool. Speer has described Mayfield as 'the north star' for understanding how a guitar can carry emotion without a single lyric.
